description: You are a database-reliability engineer who has just inherited a very small backup configuration kept under /home/user/db_backup. Inside that directory you will find two existing files that need to be edited: 1. /home/user/db_backup/config.yaml – YAML syntax 2. /home/user/db_backup/retention.toml – TOML syntax Your assignment: A. Update config.yaml so that it ends up **exactly** with the following key / value pairs (pay attention to indentation and quoting because the automated tests are strict): ``` database: host: "localhost" port: 5432 name: "maindb" backup: enabled: true schedule: "02:00" compression: "gzip" ``` B. Update retention.toml so that it ends up **exactly** with this content: ``` [retention] max_backups = 7 keep_days = 30 cold_storage = false ``` C. After making those two edits, append a plain-text changelog named /home/user/db_backup/changes.log. The log must contain **exactly two lines, in the precise order shown below, with no extra blank lines or trailing spaces**: ``` config.yaml: schedule set to 02:00, compression set to gzip retention.toml: max_backups set to 7, keep_days set to 30, cold_storage set to false ``` Guidelines & verification rules the grader will apply: • Directory path and filenames must match **exactly** (case-sensitive). • YAML indentation must be two spaces per level, no TAB characters. • TOML file must begin with the [retention] section header and have one space before and after the equals sign, except on the [retention] line which has none. • The log file must have only those two lines terminated with a single newline character at the end of the second line. Once you have finished, all three files should be present under /home/user/db_backup in the required final form so automated tests can read them verbatim.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent starts:

Directory: /home/user/db_backup (read-write for the regular user)

1. /home/user/db_backup/config.yaml  (contents)
-------------------------------------
database:
  host: "localhost"
  port: 5432
  name: "maindb"
backup:
  enabled: false
  schedule: "not_set"
  compression: "none"
-------------------------------------

2. /home/user/db_backup/retention.toml  (contents)
-------------------------------------
[retention]
max_backups = 0
keep_days   = 0
cold_storage = true
-------------------------------------

3. /home/user/db_backup/changes.log  (does not exist)

Expected final state after the task:

1. /home/user/db_backup/config.yaml  (contents)
-------------------------------------
database:
  host: "localhost"
  port: 5432
  name: "maindb"
backup:
  enabled: true
  schedule: "02:00"
  compression: "gzip"
-------------------------------------

2. /home/user/db_backup/retention.toml  (contents)
-------------------------------------
[retention]
max_backups = 7
keep_days   = 30
cold_storage = false
-------------------------------------

3. /home/user/db_backup/changes.log  (contents)
-------------------------------------
config.yaml: schedule set to 02:00, compression set to gzip
retention.toml: max_backups set to 7, keep_days set to 30, cold_storage set to false
-------------------------------------
